channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trader Joe's is opening a dozen new locations in 2025: Here's where
item.description: The company’s website currently lists 12 planned locations “opening soon” across seven states and Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Nearly all of Puerto Rico is without power on New Year's Eve
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A blackout hit nearly all of Puerto Rico early Tuesday as the U.S. territory prepared to celebrate New Year’s, leaving more than 1.3 million clients in the dark. Officials said it could take up to two days to restore power.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US levels sanctions on entities in Iran, Russia over attempted election interference
item.description: The Biden administration announced new sanctions against Russia and Iran on Tuesday in response to efforts by both countries to influence the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Have any New Year's resolutions? Here are some tips to help make them stick
item.description: Studies have shown that up to 70% of people who make New Year’s resolutions abandon those good intentions within months. Here are some tips for how to keep those resolutions when your willpower starts to falter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas teen allegedly killed rival competitor's show goat in act of jealousy
item.description: A Texas teenager is facing a felony cruelty to animals charge after she allegedly poisoned a rival competitor's 6-month-old show goat, resulting in his death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AP WAS THERE: Carter announces Middle East peace agreement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — ___ EDITORS: As part of its coverage of the death of former President Jimmy Carter, The Associated Press is looking back at major events in his life. Carter's success at Camp David negotiating a Mideast Peace agreement between Egypt and Israel was one of his crowning achievements.
item.dc:creator: BARRY SCHWEID, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The world population will be 8.09 billion on New Year's Day after a 71 million increase in 2024
item.description: The world population increased by more than 71 million people in 2024 and will be 8.09 billion people on New Year's Day, according to U.S. Census Bureau estimates released Monday. The 0.9% increase in 2024 was a slight slowdown from 2023, when the world population grew by 75 million people.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gal Gadot says she had 'massive blood clot' in her brain during pregnancy
item.description: "Wonder Woman" actor Gal Gadot revealed Sunday that she underwent emergency surgery earlier this year after a "massive blood clot" was found in her brain while she was eight months pregnant.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: UnitedHealthcare taught us ways to deny claims: Former employee
item.description: Collins described crying at her desk while handling calls from desperate patients, as supervisors laughed.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Appeals court upholds verdict finding Trump liable for sexually assaulting E. Jean Carroll
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Monday upheld a jury’s verdict finding President-elect Donald Trump liable for sexually assaulting advice columnist E. Jean Carroll and ordering him to pay $5 million.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UPDATE: Search for suspects continues in Mississippi after Ford pickup found
item.description: SATURDAY 12/28/2024 10:45 a.m. (EL PASO COUNTY, Colo.) — EPSO reported that law enforcement in Hattiesburg, Mississippi located the 1994 Ford F-150 pick-up truck early on Saturday, Dec. 28.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Eberhardt, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Korea struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Linda Lavin, Tony-winning actor who starred in the sitcom ‘Alice,’ dies at 87
item.description: Linda Lavin, a Tony Award-winning stage actor who became a working class icon as a paper-hat wearing waitress on the TV sitcom “Alice,” has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Biden uses former President Jimmy Carter's death to criticize Trump in a verbal spar and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida law will create safety barrier for first responders
item.description: A new Florida law will keep first responders safe by prohibiting a person from approaching any police, fire or EMS personnel while working on a call.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Darnold gives Vikings another gem with career-high 377 yards in 27-25 win over Packers
item.description: The Minnesota Vikings were ready to start their postgame ritual in honor of a ninth consecutive victory before realizing Sam Darnold was still missing from the raucous locker room because he was finishing his interview with the TV broadcasting crew.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US children are struggling with reading
item.description: Learning to read can become exponentially more difficult for older students and adults who have fallen behind, creating grave concerns as the number of individuals struggling with literacy into high school and adulthood is increasing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KTLA 5

item.title: The Latest: State funeral for Jimmy Carter will be Jan. 9
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter has died at the age of 100. The 39th president of the United States was a Georgia peanut farmer who sought to restore trust in government when he assumed the presidency in 1977 and then built a reputation for tireless work as a humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Political world offers condolences for Jimmy Carter
item.description: Members of the political world offered condolences and warm wishes for the family of former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday afternoon. They honored Carter’s decades of achievement and service to the country. Carter, who turned 100 earlier this year, died peacefully in Palins, Ga.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Charles Shyer, ‘Father of the Bride’ and ‘Baby Boom’ filmmaker, dies at 83
item.description: Charles Shyer, the Oscar-nominated writer and filmmaker known for classic comedies like “Private Benjamin,” “Baby Boom” and “Father of the Bride” that he made alongside Nancy Meyers, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Carter to be honored with events in Georgia, Washington
item.description: Former President Carter will be honored with public observances in both Georgia and Washington, D.C., in the coming days, following his death on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, Fox 5

item.title: Carter to be honored with events in Georgia, Washington
item.description: Former President Carter will be honored with public observances in both Georgia and Washington, D.C., in the coming days, following his death on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, KRON4

item.title: Former President Jimmy Carter dies at 100
item.description: Former President Carter, the unassuming Georgia peanut farmer whose improbable political rise took him to the governor’s mansion before the White House, died peacefully on Sunday in Plains, Ga., according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Scott Wong, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former President Jimmy Carter dies at 100 years old
item.description: James Earl Carter, Jr., the 39th President of the United States and 76th governor of Georgia, has died at 100-years-old. Known from boyhood as Jimmy, Carter rose from the dusty peanut fields of Plains, Georgia, to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Tom Ingram, WGN 9

item.title: Winning ticket for $1.22 billion lottery jackpot sold in California, Mega Millions says
item.description: At least one Mega Millions player has plenty of dough to ring in the New Year after drawing the winning number.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nasty norovirus back in full force with US cases of stomach virus surging
item.description: Cases of a wretched stomach bug are surging in parts of the United States this winter, according to government data.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: These glasses let you 'see' conversations in real time
item.description: When I heard about AI glasses that let you "see" what people are saying, I was skeptical. But after trying them on, I was proven wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KRON4

item.title: Oregon men search for Sasquatch, die in Washington forest
item.description: Two men from Portland searching for Sasquatch and missing since early Christmas morning were found dead in the Gifford Pinchot National Forest, likely from exposure and being ill-prepared.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, Fox 5

item.title: Nasty norovirus is back in full force with US cases of the stomach virus surging
item.description: Cases of a wretched stomach bug are surging in parts of the United States this winter, according to government data.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Supplemental Security Income payment schedule is wonky to start 2025: What to know
item.description: Regardless of when your payment comes, know that you will still receive all of your intended checks in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Tornadoes in Texas and Mississippi kill 2 and injure 6 as severe weather system moves east
item.description: A strong storm system threatened to whip up tornadoes in parts of the U.S. Southeast on Sunday, a day after severe weather claimed at least two lives as twisters touched down in Texas and Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: KRON4

item.title: VIDEO: Driver fleeing at 120 mph slams into trooper in Florida
item.description: A driver is facing several charges after fleeing Florida Highway Patrol troopers, and slamming into their patrol vehicle at speeds over 120 mph in Tampa
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: Big Lots reaches deal to keep hundreds of US stores open
item.description: The discount chain Big Lots, which filed for bankruptcy protection in September, has reached a deal that will keep hundreds of its stores and distribution centers open.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, KTLA 5

item.title: Explosion at Tyson Foods plant leaves 1 dead, others injured
item.description: An explosion at a Tyson Foods plant in Georgia late Thursday night left one person dead and others injured.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘contemptible fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Will New Year's Eve be loud or quiet? What are the top 2025 resolutions? AP-NORC poll has answers
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If you're planning on ringing in the new year quietly at home, you're not alone. A majority of U.S adults intend to celebrate New Year’s Eve at home, according to a new poll by The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
